Champion Enablement Kit

The internal resources to get condition monitoring approved.

ROI template, executive summary, objection-handling guide, and pilot proposal framework. Built for the person inside the organisation building the case.

Get the kit

What's in the kit

Five tools for getting internal buy-in.

You've done the evaluation. Now you need to convince procurement, finance, and leadership. This kit gives you the frameworks and data to do it.

01

ROI calculator template

Editable spreadsheet with your fleet size, failure costs, and energy spend. Produces a first-pass business case with payback period and annual savings.

02

Executive summary (1 pager)

One page. Three sections: problem, solution, proof. Designed for a VP or Director who has 5 minutes and needs the key facts.

03

Objection-handling guide

The 10 most common internal objections — "we already have vibration", "the budget's frozen", "IT won't approve it" — with data-backed responses.

04

Pilot proposal framework

A template for proposing a phased pilot: scope, timeline, success criteria, and resource requirements. Adapted for 3-month and 6-month options.

05

Industry proof summaries

One-page proof summaries for water, steel, chemicals, airports, and oil & gas. Each includes validated results, customer names, and quantified outcomes.

Who this is for

The person inside who already believes — and needs others to.

Reliability managers

You've evaluated the technology. Now you need to present the business case to leadership and get budget approved.

Operations directors

You see the fleet-wide opportunity. You need a structured proposal that finance and procurement will accept.

Innovation leads

You're sponsoring the pilot. You need ready-made materials to align stakeholders across engineering, IT, and finance.

Get the Champion Enablement Kit

We'll send the full kit within 1 business day. Your data stays private.

✓

Kit on its way.

We'll send the Champion Enablement Kit within 1 business day. While you wait, try the ROI calculator to get a first estimate for your fleet.
